title: Understanding the Relationship
text: To find the torque produced by the turbine, we need to understand the relationship between power, torque, and angular velocity.

title: Formula for Power
text: The power output of a rotating object is given by the formula: Power $$=$$ Torque * Angular Velocity.

title: Converting Units
text: The angular velocity is given in revolutions per minute $$\frac{rev}{min}$$. Convert this to radians per second $$\frac{rad}{s}$$ for use in the formula.

title: Calculate Angular Velocity
text: Convert $$20$$ $$\frac{rev}{min}$$ to $$\frac{rad}{s}$$. Use the conversion: $$1$$ rev $$=$$ $$2\pi$$ rad and $$1$$ min $$=$$ $$60$$ s.

title: Rearrange the Formula
text: Rearrange the formula to solve for torque: Torque $$=$$ Power / Angular Velocity.

title: Calculate Torque
text: Substitute the values into the formula: Power $$=$$ $$2.0$$ MW $$=$$ $$2\times{10}^6$$ W, Angular Velocity $$=$$ $$\frac{2\pi\times20}{60}$$ $$\frac{rad}{s}$$.
